story_title: Strait of Hormuz / Project Freedom paused
story_one_liner: America says it just won the Iran war. Iran says America just lost. So which one is true?
voice_style: BBC-broadcaster cadence, faster on setup, slower on paradox
music_brief: ambient drone bed; intro sting handles open

world_tickers.headline: Japan to buy 20m UAE oil bypassing Hormuz
US trade gap widens as oil exports surge
Lufthansa fuel bill +€1.7B on Iran war
Pyongyang gas now pricier than Seoul

scenes.voiceover: America just signed a deal to end the Iran war. They say they won. Iran says it was a shameful U.S. defeat. So who's right?
Same one-page memo. Same eleven percent oil drop. Five countries told this very differently.
Wire services kept it neutral. Trump pauses operation. Iran says safe passage possible.
scenes.headline_quoted: U.S. and Iran closing in on memorandum to end war.

scenes.voiceover: America heard victory and threat in one breath.
scenes.headline_quoted: If they don't agree, the bombing starts at a much higher level than before.
Beijing will play a greater role in ending Mideast fighting.
The United States has suffered a shameful defeat.

scenes.voiceover: And then the paradox. The U.S. establishment magazine of foreign policy. And Russian state television. Both said the same thing.

paradox_top.quote: Iran proved it can close the strait, even in the face of significant military force.

paradox_bottom.quote: The reality in the Strait contradicts U.S. claims. Tehran still holds the strategic edge.

scenes.voiceover: Same facts. Five stories. Could every one of them be telling the truth?
scenes.on_screen_text:
Same facts.
Five stories.
Daily Framings — follow.

fact_check_provenance.primary_event_source: Pakistani diplomatic source via Reuters, confirmed by Axios

Beijing to play 'greater role' in ending Mideast fighting

Iran's New Oil Weapon — Iran proved it can close the strait, even in the face of significant military force
